{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|12=draft}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr|12=draft}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|12=draft}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson korin|en}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=draft}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|12=draft}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|12=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=draft}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />

After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />

However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|12=draft}} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Expand Japanese|建比良鳥命|建比良鳥命=建比良鳥命}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Takehi-Nateru | image =File:Washinomiya Shrine - new gate.jpg | god_of = [[Birds|Birds]] | father = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a" /><ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> | consort = | siblings = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en}}<ref name="Brown-1988" />|relatives = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (uncle) [[Amatsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Ikutsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Kumanokusubi]] (uncle)
|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=draft}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=draft}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|12=draft}}}} {{complex}}
<!-- {{AFC comment|1=took from {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} {{ill|User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le|en|12=draft}} ❤️💚💙 {{ill|User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)|en|12=draft}} 05:09, 5 May 2023 (UTC)}} -->
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|12=draft}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr|12=draft}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|12=draft}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson korin|en}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=draft}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|12=draft}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|12=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=draft}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|12=draft}} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru]]

After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />

|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=draft}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=draft}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|12=draft}}}} {{complex}}
<!-- {{AFC comment|1=took from {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} {{ill|User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le|en|12=draft}} ❤️💚💙 {{ill|User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)|en|12=draft}} 05:09, 5 May 2023 (UTC)}} -->
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|12=draft}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr|12=draft}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|12=draft}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson korin|en}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=draft}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|12=draft}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|12=draft}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|12=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=draft}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|12=draft}} [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 建比良鳥命 | 世代名 = | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 建比良鳥命（タケヒラトリノミコト） | 別名 = 武夷鳥、天夷鳥、武日照命、天夷鳥命、天日名鳥命、武雛命 | 神階 = | 神格 = [[鳥]]神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[天之菩卑能命]] | 母 = | 親 = | 兄弟姉妹 = | 配偶者 = | 子 =[[伊佐我命]] 等 | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[鷲宮神社]] * [[比奈多乃神社]] * [[道明寺天満宮#摂末社|元宮土師社]] 等 }} |記紀等=[[古事記]]、[[日本書紀]]、[[延喜式]] 等|関連氏族=[[出雲国造]]、[[无邪志国造]]、[[上菟上国造]]、[[下菟上国造]]、[[伊自牟国造]]、[[津島県直]]、[[遠江国造]] 等}} [[Image:Children born from the Challenge of Amaterasu and Susanoo-ja.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（古事記に基づく） [[メディア:Children born from the Challenge of Amaterasu and Susanoo-ja.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''建比良鳥命'''（タケヒラトリ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』では'''建比良鳥命'''、『日本書紀』では'''大背飯三熊之大人'''（オオソビノミクマノウシ）・'''武三熊之大人'''（タケミクマノウシ）・'''武日照命'''（タケヒナテル）・'''武夷鳥'''（タケヒナトリ） ・'''天夷鳥'''（アマノヒナトリ）、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では'''天夷鳥命'''、'''天日名鳥命'''と表記される。これらの異名・異称の同定は出雲国造家として[[出雲大社]]の祭祀を受け継いだ[[出雲国造#千家家|千家家]]が伝える系譜書『出雲國造傳統略』に拠っている{{Sfn|千家|1882}}。 建比良鳥命の「建」は「勇猛な」、「比良」は、「縁（へり）」と同源であり、物の端・隣との境界の意と解し、名義は「勇猛な、異郷への境界を飛ぶ鳥」と考えられる。「[[黄泉比良坂]]」の「比良」も、[[黄泉国]]と[[現世|現し国]]との境界を指し、「山城の幣羅坂」の「幣羅」も村の境界、[[奥津甲斐弁羅神]]の「弁羅」も海と陸地の境界である。また[[鳥取神]]や[[鳥鳴海神]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]同様、鳥が人間の霊魂を異郷に運ぶという信仰に基づき、名義の「異郷への境界」は[[出雲国]]との境界と考えられる{{Sfn|西宮|2014|pp=364-365}}。 また別名の天夷鳥命の名義は「[[高天原]]から 夷（鄙・ひな=出雲国）へ飛び下った鳥」の意であるから、建比良鳥命と同一の神格と考えられる{{Sfn|西宮|2014|pp=364-365}}。 == 記述 == 『古事記』では[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約|天照大御神と須佐之男命の誓約]]の段で、[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]]の子が建比良鳥命であり、出雲国造・无邪志国造・上菟上国造・下菟上国造・伊自牟国造・津島県直・遠江国造等の祖神であると記されている。 {{Quotation|天菩比命之子、建比良鳥命 【此出雲國造无耶志國造上菟上國造下菟上國造伊自牟國造津嶋縣直遠江國造等之祖也】}} 『日本書紀』では、[[葦原中国]]の平定の段で大背飯三熊之大人（武三熊之大人ともいう）が父の天穂日命に次いで葦原中国に派遣されたが、父と同様に何も報告して来なかったと記されている。また、[[崇神天皇]]60年7月、天皇が「武日照命（武夷鳥とも天夷鳥ともいう）が天から持って来た[[神宝]]が[[出雲大社]]に納められているから、それを見たい」と言って献上させ、その結果出雲氏に内紛が起き、当時の当主の[[出雲振根]]が誅殺されたとも記されている。 『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』に[[天安 (日本)|天安]]二年三月二十二日条に「在河内國天夷鳥命神授従五位下」とあり、現在の[[道明寺天満宮]]に祭られる天夷鳥命に従五位下が授けられたと考えられる。 {{Quotation|天照太神之子。正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穗耳尊。娶高皇產靈尊之女栲幡千千姫。生天津火瓊瓊杵尊。故皇祖高皇產靈尊。特鍾憐愛以崇養焉。遂欲立皇孫天津火瓊瓊杵尊以爲葦原中國之主。然彼地多有螢火光神及蝿聲邪神。復有草木咸能言語。故高皇產靈尊召集八十諸神。而問之曰。吾欲令撥平葦原中國之邪鬼。當遣誰者宜也。惟爾諸神勿隱所知。僉曰。天穗日命是神之傑也。可不試歟。於是、俯順衆言。即以天穗日命往平之。然此神侫媚於大己貴神。比及三年尚不報聞。故仍遣其子大背飯三熊之大人。〈大人。此云于志。〉亦名武三熊之大人。此亦還順其父。遂不報聞。|巻第二「神代下」}} {{Quotation|《崇神天皇六〇年（癸未前三八）七月己酉（十四）》六十年秋七月丙申朔己酉。詔群臣曰。武日照命。〈　一云。武夷鳥。又云。天夷鳥。　〉従天将来神宝。蔵于出雲大神宮。是欲見焉。則遣矢田部造遠祖武諸隅、〈　一書云。一名大母隅也。　〉而使献。当是時。出雲臣之遠祖出雲振根主于神宝。是往筑紫国而不遇矣。其弟飯入根則被皇命。以神宝、付弟甘美韓日狭与子鸕濡渟而貢上。既而出雲振根従筑紫還来之。聞神宝献于朝廷。責其弟飯入根曰。数日当待。何恐之乎。輒許神宝。是以既経年月。猶懐恨忿、有殺弟之志。仍欺弟曰。頃者於止屋淵多生菨。願共行欲見。則随兄而往之。先是。兄窃作木刀。形似真刀。当時自佩之。弟佩真刀。共到淵頭。兄謂弟曰。淵水清冷。願欲共游沐。弟従兄言。各解佩刀、置淵辺。沐於水中。乃兄先上陸。取弟真刀自佩。後弟驚而取兄木刀。共相撃矣。弟不得抜木刀。兄撃弟飯入根而殺之。故時人歌之曰。 椰句毛多菟　伊頭毛多鶏流餓　波鶏流多知　菟頭邏佐波磨枳　佐微那辞珥　阿波礼やくもたつ　いづもたけるが　はけるたち　つづらさはまき　さみなしに　あはれ　（日本書紀歌謡020）
於是甘美韓日狭。鸕濡渟。参向朝廷、曲奏其状。則遣吉備津彦与武渟河別。以誅出雲振根。故出雲臣等畏是事。不祭大神而有間。時丹波氷上人。名氷香戸辺。啓于皇太子活目尊曰。己子有小児。而自然言之。 玉菨鎮石。出雲人祭。真種之甘美鏡。押羽振。甘美御神、底宝御宝主。山河之水泳御魂。静挂甘美御神、底宝御宝主也。〈　菨。此云毛。　〉 是非似小児之言。若有託言乎。於是。皇太子奏于天皇。則勅之使祭。|巻第十四「崇神紀」}} 『延喜式』巻八の「[[出雲国造神賀詞]]」には、「天夷鳥命に[[経津主神|布都怒志命]]を副へて天降し」という一節がある。 == 系譜 == 『古事記』『日本書紀』等によれば、[[天照大御神]]の五男子の一柱である天之菩卑能命の子とされる。出雲国造系図では次代を伊佐我命（櫛瓊命）とする{{Sfn|千家|1882}}。 == 祀る神社 == * 鷲宮神社（[[埼玉県]][[久喜市]]） * 比奈多乃神社（[[静岡県]][[掛川市]]） * 元宮土師社（[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]・[[道明寺天満宮]]境内） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === === 出典 === == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=出雲國造傳統略 |date=1882年4月 |publisher=千家武主 |ref={{SfnRef|千家|1882}} |author=千家武主 謹編 |ncid=BA55648765}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == * [[キヒサツミ]] - 『古事記』[[垂仁天皇]]条で出雲国造の祖とされる。 == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/takehiratorinomikoto/ 建比良鳥命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [http://www.washinomiyajinja.or.jp/history/history.html 鷲宮神社　神社由緒　関東最古の大社・お酉様の本社] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4409208 比奈多乃神社 - 静岡県神社庁] * [https://www.domyojitenmangu.com/hajisha.html 道明寺天満宮　元宮　土師社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけひらとり}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:国造]]

『古事記』では'''建比良鳥命'''、『日本書紀』では'''大背飯三熊之大人'''（オオソビノミクマノウシ）・'''武三熊之大人'''（タケミクマノウシ）・'''武日照命'''（タケヒナテル）・'''武夷鳥'''（タケヒナトリ） ・'''天夷鳥'''（アマノヒナトリ）、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では'''天夷鳥命'''、'''天日名鳥命'''と表記される。これらの異名・異称の同定は出雲国造家として[[出雲大社]]の祭祀を受け継いだ[[出雲国造#千家家|千家家]]が伝える系譜書『出雲國造傳統略』に拠っている{{Sfn|千家|1882}}。 建比良鳥命の「建」は「勇猛な」、「比良」は、「縁（へり）」と同源であり、物の端・隣との境界の意と解し、名義は「勇猛な、異郷への境界を飛ぶ鳥」と考えられる。「[[黄泉比良坂]]」の「比良」も、[[黄泉国]]と[[現世|現し国]]との境界を指し、「山城の幣羅坂」の「幣羅」も村の境界、[[奥津甲斐弁羅神]]の「弁羅」も海と陸地の境界である。また[[鳥取神]]や[[鳥鳴海神]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]同様、鳥が人間の霊魂を異郷に運ぶという信仰に基づき、名義の「異郷への境界」は[[出雲国]]との境界と考えられる{{Sfn|西宮|2014|pp=364-365}}。 また別名の天夷鳥命の名義は「[[高天原]]から 夷（鄙・ひな=出雲国）へ飛び下った鳥」の意であるから、建比良鳥命と同一の神格と考えられる{{Sfn|西宮|2014|pp=364-365}}。 == 記述 == 『古事記』では[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約|天照大御神と須佐之男命の誓約]]の段で、[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]]の子が建比良鳥命であり、出雲国造・无邪志国造・上菟上国造・下菟上国造・伊自牟国造・津島県直・遠江国造等の祖神であると記されている。 {{Quotation|天菩比命之子、建比良鳥命 【此出雲國造无耶志國造上菟上國造下菟上國造伊自牟國造津嶋縣直遠江國造等之祖也】}} 『日本書紀』では、[[葦原中国]]の平定の段で大背飯三熊之大人（武三熊之大人ともいう）が父の天穂日命に次いで葦原中国に派遣されたが、父と同様に何も報告して来なかったと記されている。また、[[崇神天皇]]60年7月、天皇が「武日照命（武夷鳥とも天夷鳥ともいう）が天から持って来た[[神宝]]が[[出雲大社]]に納められているから、それを見たい」と言って献上させ、その結果出雲氏に内紛が起き、当時の当主の[[出雲振根]]が誅殺されたとも記されている。 『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』に[[天安 (日本)|天安]]二年三月二十二日条に「在河内國天夷鳥命神授従五位下」とあり、現在の[[道明寺天満宮]]に祭られる天夷鳥命に従五位下が授けられたと考えられる。 {{Quotation|天照太神之子。正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穗耳尊。娶高皇產靈尊之女栲幡千千姫。生天津火瓊瓊杵尊。故皇祖高皇產靈尊。特鍾憐愛以崇養焉。遂欲立皇孫天津火瓊瓊杵尊以爲葦原中國之主。然彼地多有螢火光神及蝿聲邪神。復有草木咸能言語。故高皇產靈尊召集八十諸神。而問之曰。吾欲令撥平葦原中國之邪鬼。當遣誰者宜也。惟爾諸神勿隱所知。僉曰。天穗日命是神之傑也。可不試歟。於是、俯順衆言。即以天穗日命往平之。然此神侫媚於大己貴神。比及三年尚不報聞。故仍遣其子大背飯三熊之大人。〈大人。此云于志。〉亦名武三熊之大人。此亦還順其父。遂不報聞。|巻第二「神代下」}} {{Quotation|《崇神天皇六〇年（癸未前三八）七月己酉（十四）》六十年秋七月丙申朔己酉。詔群臣曰。武日照命。〈　一云。武夷鳥。又云。天夷鳥。　〉従天将来神宝。蔵于出雲大神宮。是欲見焉。則遣矢田部造遠祖武諸隅、〈　一書云。一名大母隅也。　〉而使献。当是時。出雲臣之遠祖出雲振根主于神宝。是往筑紫国而不遇矣。其弟飯入根則被皇命。以神宝、付弟甘美韓日狭与子鸕濡渟而貢上。既而出雲振根従筑紫還来之。聞神宝献于朝廷。責其弟飯入根曰。数日当待。何恐之乎。輒許神宝。是以既経年月。猶懐恨忿、有殺弟之志。仍欺弟曰。頃者於止屋淵多生菨。願共行欲見。則随兄而往之。先是。兄窃作木刀。形似真刀。当時自佩之。弟佩真刀。共到淵頭。兄謂弟曰。淵水清冷。願欲共游沐。弟従兄言。各解佩刀、置淵辺。沐於水中。乃兄先上陸。取弟真刀自佩。後弟驚而取兄木刀。共相撃矣。弟不得抜木刀。兄撃弟飯入根而殺之。故時人歌之曰。 椰句毛多菟　伊頭毛多鶏流餓　波鶏流多知　菟頭邏佐波磨枳　佐微那辞珥　阿波礼やくもたつ　いづもたけるが　はけるたち　つづらさはまき　さみなしに　あはれ　（日本書紀歌謡020）
於是甘美韓日狭。鸕濡渟。参向朝廷、曲奏其状。則遣吉備津彦与武渟河別。以誅出雲振根。故出雲臣等畏是事。不祭大神而有間。時丹波氷上人。名氷香戸辺。啓于皇太子活目尊曰。己子有小児。而自然言之。 玉菨鎮石。出雲人祭。真種之甘美鏡。押羽振。甘美御神、底宝御宝主。山河之水泳御魂。静挂甘美御神、底宝御宝主也。〈　菨。此云毛。　〉 是非似小児之言。若有託言乎。於是。皇太子奏于天皇。則勅之使祭。|巻第十四「崇神紀」}} 『延喜式』巻八の「[[出雲国造神賀詞]]」には、「天夷鳥命に[[経津主神|布都怒志命]]を副へて天降し」という一節がある。 == 系譜 == 『古事記』『日本書紀』等によれば、[[天照大御神]]の五男子の一柱である天之菩卑能命の子とされる。出雲国造系図では次代を伊佐我命（櫛瓊命）とする{{Sfn|千家|1882}}。 == 祀る神社 == * 鷲宮神社（[[埼玉県]][[久喜市]]） * 比奈多乃神社（[[静岡県]][[掛川市]]） * 元宮土師社（[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]・[[道明寺天満宮]]境内） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === === 出典 === == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=出雲國造傳統略 |date=1882年4月 |publisher=千家武主 |ref={{SfnRef|千家|1882}} |author=千家武主 謹編 |ncid=BA55648765}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == * [[キヒサツミ]] - 『古事記』[[垂仁天皇]]条で出雲国造の祖とされる。 == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/takehiratorinomikoto/ 建比良鳥命 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [http://www.washinomiyajinja.or.jp/history/history.html 鷲宮神社　神社由緒　関東最古の大社・お酉様の本社] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4409208 比奈多乃神社 - 静岡県神社庁] * [https://www.domyojitenmangu.com/hajisha.html 道明寺天満宮　元宮　土師社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけひらとり}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:国造]]

The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=took from {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}
{{Expand Japanese|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|建比良鳥命=建比良鳥命}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Takehi-Nateru | image =File:Washinomiya Shrine - new gate.jpg | god_of = [[Birds|Birds]] | father = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a" /><ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> | consort = | siblings = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="Brown-1988" />|relatives = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (uncle) [[Amatsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Ikutsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Kumanokusubi]] (uncle)
|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}}}
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson korin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson korin|qq=}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. {{Expand Japanese|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|建比良鳥命=建比良鳥命}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Takehi-Nateru | image =File:Washinomiya Shrine - new gate.jpg | god_of = [[Birds|Birds]] | father = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a" /><ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> | consort = | siblings = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="Brown-1988" />|relatives = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (uncle) [[Amatsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Ikutsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Kumanokusubi]] (uncle)
|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}}}
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson korin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson korin|qq=}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{Expand Japanese|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|建比良鳥命=建比良鳥命}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Takehi-Nateru | image =File:Washinomiya Shrine - new gate.jpg | god_of = [[:en:Birds|Birds]] | father = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a" /><ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> | consort = | siblings = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="Brown-1988" />|relatives = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (uncle) [[Amatsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Ikutsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Kumanokusubi]] (uncle)
|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}}}
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson korin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson korin|qq=}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{Expand Japanese|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|建比良鳥命=建比良鳥命}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Takehi-Nateru | image =File:Washinomiya Shrine - new gate.jpg | god_of = [[:en:Birds|Birds]] | father = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a" /><ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> | consort = | siblings = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="Brown-1988" />|relatives = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (uncle) [[Amatsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Ikutsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Kumanokusubi]] (uncle)
|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}}}
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson korin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson korin|qq=}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=took from {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}
{{Expand Japanese|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|建比良鳥命=建比良鳥命}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Takehi-Nateru | image =File:Washinomiya Shrine - new gate.jpg | god_of = [[:en:Birds|Birds]] | father = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a" /><ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> | consort = | siblings = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="Brown-1988" />|relatives = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (uncle) [[Amatsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Ikutsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Kumanokusubi]] (uncle)
|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}}}
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson korin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson korin|qq=}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[reed bed]]s''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehi-Nateru]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=took from {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}
{{Expand Japanese|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|建比良鳥命=建比良鳥命}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Takehi-Nateru | image =File:Washinomiya Shrine - new gate.jpg | god_of = [[:en:Birds|Birds]] | father = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a" /><ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> | consort = | siblings = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="Brown-1988" />|relatives = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (uncle) [[Amatsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Ikutsuhikone]] (uncle) [[Kumanokusubi]] (uncle)
|cult_centre={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}|member_of=[[Izumo clan]]|caption={{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, a cult center of {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}}}
{{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}} (建比良鳥命) is a Japanese god and son of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-03-21|title=Takehiratori {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230321165525/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9146|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="web.archive.org-2023a">{{Cite web|date=2023-06-05|title=Amenohohi {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605104153/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9434|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="shintoshrines">{{Cite web|title=The Gods of Japan {{!}} Amaterasu-Ōkami 天照大御神 {{!}}Susanoo-no-Mikoto 須佐之男命|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/amasusa|access-date=2024-03-24|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}</ref><ref name="More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan">{{Cite web|title=More glimpses of unfamiliar Japan|url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2014/03/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=en}}</ref><ref name="英訳 出雲神話">{{Cite web|title=英訳 出雲神話/English Translation Izumo Myths::２．天の安の河の誓約-出雲神話翻訳研究会/The Man From Izumo tells the Izumo-myths of the Kojiki|url=http://izumo-kojiki.com/izumomyths/amaterasu/100/|access-date=2024-03-24|language=ja}}</ref> one of the five male kami (divine beings) born from a special event known as the "trial by pledge" between two major deities, Amaterasu and Susanoo. {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was sent to the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} to prepare for the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson kōrin|lt=Tenson korin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson korin|qq=}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
He is the ancestor of many prominent families in, [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]], [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba]], and [[Tsushima Province|Tsushima]].<ref name="shintoshrines" /><ref name="英訳 出雲神話" /> He started the Fire fesival of [[Izumo-taisha]].<ref name="Brown-1988" />
Ame no Hohi's other son is {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|qq=}}.<ref name="Brown-1988">{{Cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer M. |url=https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Cambridge_History_of_Japan/A3_6lp8IOK8C?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=Ame+no+Hohi&pg=PA346&printsec=frontcover |title=The Cambridge History of Japan |last2=Hall |first2=John Whitney |last3=Brown |first3=Delmer Myers |last4=Press |first4=Cambridge University |last5=Jansen |first5=Marius B. |last6=McCullough |first6=William H. |last7=Shively |first7=Donald H. |last8=Yamamura |first8=Kozo |last9=Duus |first9=Peter |date=1988 |publisher=Cambridge University Press |isbn=978-0-521-22352-2 |pages=346 |language=en}}</ref> He is also known by the name {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|en|Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Ame-no-Hinadori|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinadori|qq=}}, contrary to popular belief that is not the name of an additional son.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Names == Takehi-Nateru, also known by several other names such as Takehiratori no Mikoto, Takehinatori no Mikoto, and Ame no Hinatori.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Role in Mythology == In the Kojiki, the oldest Japanese book, Takehi-Nateru is mentioned as an ancestor to the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Both the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], narrate how {{ill|Ame no Hohi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} was tasked with preparing the {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of [[:en:Reed bed|Reed bed]]s''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but ultimately did not succeed in his mission.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
So Takehi-Nateru and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} went down to pacify {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. They managed to subdue unruly deities and persuaded a prominent deity, [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi]], to leave the earthly realm. After their victory the heavenly grandchild [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] descended to the Earth and Takehi-Nateru became the ancestor of the [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], who ruled [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]].<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== Legacy == According to the Nihongi's account of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], Takehi-Nateru descended from heaven with divine treasures.<ref name="Brown-1988" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023" /> These treasures were kept at [[Izumo-taisha]] and later handed over to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. However, this transfer led to conflicts among the Izumo, Furune, and Iiirine family branches, who were the keepers of these sacred items. Today, shrines in [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo]] and [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba]] honor Takehi-Nateru.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
== References == {{reflist|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
